findings.evidence: Line 110 declares `lemma IsEffective.coeff_eq_zero_of_notMem_support ... (_ : IsEffective D)`, and line 112 proves it solely by `Finsupp.notMem_support_iff.mp hx`; Mathlib already has `Finsupp.notMem_support_iff` at `Mathlib/Data/Finsupp/Defs.lean:163`.
findings.fix: Move it out of the `IsEffective` namespace as a general divisor support lemma, e.g. `lemma coeff_eq_zero_of_notMem_support {D : WeilDivisor X} {x : X} (hx : x ∉ D.support) : coeff D x = 0`, near the coefficient/support API.
findings.issue: `IsEffective.coeff_eq_zero_of_notMem_support` is in the `IsEffective` namespace and takes an unused `IsEffective D` hypothesis, but the statement does not depend on effectivity.

summary: One new lemma is placed under the effectivity API even though it is just the general Finsupp support characterization for any divisor. The rest of the added degree and weighted-degree material is in the natural `WeilDivisor` file next to the definitions it elaborates.
